task: I’ve got a place that needs a proper once-over before I call it done, and I don’t want to miss anything obvious. What’s a solid checklist or sequence to follow so the job comes out right the first time?

turns.agent_response:
Use this quick order so you catch the obvious stuff first:

**1) Start high**
- Dust/wipe tops, shelves, vents, frames, lights
- Check corners, ledges, behind doors

**2) Work down**
- Walls, switches, handles, counters
- Tables, desks, sinks, mirrors
- Then floors last: vacuum/sweep, mop if needed

**3) Check the “touch points”**
- Door handles
- Light switches
- Rails
- Remote controls / phones / buttons
- Faucets and fixtures

**4) Look for misses**
- Spots, streaks, crumbs, hair, trash
- Smudges on glass/mirrors
- Anything out of place or broken

**5) Finish with a quick walk-through**
- Open/close doors
- Turn lights on/off
- Make sure nothing was left behind
- Confirm the room looks clean from the entrance

**If something looks off:** note it right away instead of waiting until the end.

If you want, I can turn this into a **tiny start / middle / end checklist** you can use fast in office or clinic rooms.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the right order. I’d want it a little tighter though — something I can run the same way every time, no thinking.

Maybe split it like this:
- **Before you start:** trash out, supplies ready, lights on, check what room it is
- **Top to bottom:** dust high spots, then wipe surfaces, then touch points
- **Floors last:** vacuum/sweep, mop if needed, corners too
- **Final pass:** glass, mirrors, sinks, handles, under desks, behind doors
- **Walk-through:** look from the doorway, make sure nothing got missed

One thing I always check is the stuff people actually touch. That’s where the dirt shows fastest.

If you want, make it for **office rooms** or **medical rooms** separate. Those aren’t quite the same.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **same-every-time sequence**.

## Office rooms
**Before you start**
- Trash out
- Supplies ready
- Lights on
- Quick look: anything broken / missing / out of place

**Top to bottom**
- Dust high spots
- Wipe surfaces
- Clean touch points: handles, switches, rails, phones, remotes

**Floors last**
- Sweep/vacuum
- Mop if needed
- Hit corners and edges

**Final pass**
- Glass / mirrors
- Under desks
- Behind doors
- Sink / counters if present

**Walk-through**
- Stand at the doorway
- Look for missed spots, streaks, crumbs, or clutter

## Medical / clinic rooms
**Before you start**
- Trash out
- Supplies ready
- Check room type / isolation / special instructions
- Lights on

**Top to bottom**
- Dust high spots
- Wipe all surfaces
- Disinfect touch points: handles, switches, rails, exam surfaces, buttons

**Floors last**
- Sweep/vacuum
- Mop if needed
- Corners and edges

**Final pass**
- Sink / faucets
- Mirrors / glass
- Under furniture
- Behind doors
- Anything visibly touched or soiled

**Walk-through**
- Doorway check
- Make sure the room looks clean and nothing was missed

If you want, I can turn this into a **super-short 1-line checklist** you can keep on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s it. Tight enough to use without thinking.

The office vs clinic split makes sense too — especially the touch points and the room check before you start. That’s the part that saves you from getting burned later.

A one-line phone version would be useful, but this already works.
